char.name: Eliezer of Damascus
char.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.

friends.name: Resh
friends.bio: Herdsman in the camp of Abram. We follow the man and his God across this land, tent to tent, well to well. He treats his servants well, and the flocks are fat this season. Praise be.

friends.name: Lirit
friends.bio: Midwife to the women of Abraham's camp. When old Sarah laughed at the very thought of a baby, well, I've delivered a few 'impossible' ones in my time. Send for me when your time comes, and don't be afraid. 🤍

friends.name: Mavah
friends.bio: Weaver and tentmaker in Abraham's camp. New goat-hair panels woven for the big tent before the cold sets in. Slow work, good work. A strong tent is a happy household.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Sarai
friends.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

posts.author: CHAR_115
posts.body: I want to tell you exactly how God works, because I watched Him do it today. I came to the well outside the city of Nahor at evening and I prayed a bold prayer: LORD, let the girl You have chosen be the one who offers water to me AND to all my camels. Before I had even finished praying, before the amen, a young woman named Rebekah came down with her jar, gave me a drink, and then said, I will draw for your camels too, until they have finished. Ten camels. Do you know how much water ten thirsty camels drink? She ran that jar to the trough until they were done. I bowed my head right there and worshiped. He sent His angel before me, exactly as the master said. Exactly.
posts.highlight: Eliezer's prayer at the well answered before he finished; Rebekah waters the camels (Gen 24:10-27)
posts.ember: God's quiet providence guiding a servant's steps; the bride prepared for the son

comments.author: CHAR_668
comments.body: I was right there at the well when it happened. She just KEPT drawing water, jar after jar after jar, for a stranger and his ten camels, and none of us knew we were watching a life change. Rebekah, you have no idea what you said yes to today.

author_info.name: Adina
author_info.bio: A girl of Haran, by the well. Rebekah is my dearest friend, and you will NOT believe it, a stranger's servant turned up with ten camels and gold, and now she's leaving to marry a man she's never even met, off in Canaan! 😱 So happy for her. Going to miss her terribly.

author_info.name: Eliezer of Damascus
author_info.bio: Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.
